name: wifi-recon
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi-recon`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: sudo python3 analyzer.py  (hardcoded: 10s tshark capture on interface wlan1mon, output to capture-dir/pcapture, then parse + store to ap-database.db)

examples.command: sudo python3 analyzer.py  (hardcoded: 10s tshark capture on interface wlan1mon, output to capture-dir/pcapture, then parse + store to ap-database.db)

dependencies: wireless adapter in monitor mode (hardcoded wlan1mon)
algorithms: Passive 802.11 frame capture via tshark on a monitor-mode interface (fixed-duration pcap)
Beacon frame (subtype 0x0008) field extraction via tshark -Tfields: BSSID, SSID, supported/extended rates, radiotap dBm signal, ESS capability flag, RSN group/pairwise cipher, AKM auth type
Probe-response frame (subtype 0x0005) parsing for associated-client enumeration (AP MAC / AP SSID / probing client MAC)
802.11 supported-rate code to Mbps decoding (rate table mapping)
RSN cipher-suite code decoding (TKIP, AES-CCMP) and AKM auth-type decoding (PSK)
OUI vendor lookup via online macvendors.com REST API (rate-limited 1s/query)
SQLite persistence of AP records keyed by BSSID with dedup via UNIQUE constraint
produces: SQLite database ap-database.db (table ap_data: bssid PK, ssid, supported/extended rates in Mbps, signal dBm, transmitter type AP/STA, group cipher, pairwise cipher(s), auth type(s))
Console report of discovered APs and associated STAs
beacon-data / probe-response-data intermediate text files (deduped tshark -Tfields output)
novelty: none — thin wrapper. The entire pipeline is `os.system("tshark ...")` calls: capture.py wraps tshark capture, frameprocessor.py wraps tshark -Tfields extraction of beacons/probe-responses with sort|uniq dedup, featureparse.py decodes the resulting text fields. airodump-ng already emits BSSID/SSID/signal/cipher/auth/OUI in a richer form with live display; this adds only (a) SQLite persistence and (b) an online macvendors.com OUI lookup (KFIOSA already has OUI enrichment). Probe-response parsing is a weaker subset of airodump-ng's client enumeration. No PMKID, no EAPOL/handshake capture, no deauth, no hidden-SSID reveal, no GPS, no channel hopping. It is a tshark-based reimplementation of basic airodump-ng output, not a new algorithm.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
